title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. pinifolia
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. pinifolia, also known as Harebell or Bluebell of Scotland, is a flowering plant in the bellflower family native to Europe, Asia, and North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Campanula rotundifolia subsp. pinifolia include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
